claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

First, let me review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have a son named Alex who is 17 years old and lives at home with you.

Since Alex is 17, I need to ask: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(This could include things like A-levels, T-levels, NVQ Level 3 or below, or other approved courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is! Alex is studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great! Since Alex is 17 and in approved education (A-levels), that meets the age requirement for Child Benefit.

Now I need to ask: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you. Now I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Does Alex work, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work. He's just focused on his studies at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One final question: **Does Alex receive any benefits in his own name, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 17 years old and studying for his A-levels, which counts as approved education
✓ He lives at home with you in the UK (Manchester)
✓ You are responsible for him
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
✓ He doesn't work 24 hours or more per week
✓ He doesn't receive benefits in his own name

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can continue to claim Child Benefit for Alex while he remains in approved education or training until he turns 20.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you might have to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (studying A-levels). According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit continues for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives at home with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex does not work 24 hours or more per week, and he does not receive any benefits in his own name such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for their 17-year-old son Alex, who lives at home with them. Alex is currently studying for his A-levels, which is approved education, and does not work or receive benefits in his own name. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.
